questions.q: What is the main advantage of using a linked list over an array?
questions.answer: Dynamic memory allocation and efficient insertion/deletion at the beginning/end.

questions.q: In a singly linked list, what is the term for the memory location that stores the address of the next node?
What is the time complexity of traversing a linked list from the beginning to the end?
Which of the following operations can be performed efficiently in a linked list?
Explain the concept of circular linked list with a suitable example.
questions.answer: A circular linked list is a type of linked list where the last node points back to the first node, forming a circular structure. Example: A playlist where the last song points back to the first song.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of traversing a linked list from the beginning to the end?
Which of the following is a disadvantage of using a linked list?
How can you implement a doubly linked list in C++?
questions.answer: You can implement a doubly linked list in C++ by using two pointers, one for the next node and one for the previous node, and defining two constructors, one for initializing the list and one for creating a new node.

questions.q: What is the time complexity of inserting a new node at the beginning of a linked list?
Which of the following operations is not supported in a singly linked list?
questions.answer: Traversal from the end to the beginning
